channel.title: AI & Tech News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregated AI and Tech news from 11 sources

item.title: IAB proposes new payment rules for AI content access
item.description: [MarTech] The IAB Tech Lab’s new protocol aims to create payment rails for AI systems using publisher content, potentially reshaping how information is valued online. The post IAB proposes new payment rules for AI content access appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Singapore-based Stryv acquires water purifier brand Sterra
item.description: [Business Times] Roy Ang believes both companies can hit their goal of achieving an overall nine-figure revenue in 2027

item.title: Human brain cells run new data centres in Singapore, Melbourne
item.description: [Business Times] AI is driving a surge in electricity demand, forcing governments and technology companies to search for more efficient computing systems

item.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Anthropic’s DOD Lawsuit, War Memes, and AI Coming for VC Jobs
item.description: [WIRED AI] In today’s episode, we discuss how the saga between Anthropic and the Department of Defense is far from over.

item.title: Grammarly pulls AI author-impersonation tool after backlash
item.description: [BBC Technology] Writers criticised the feature which used their names and styles as "AI personas" without consent.

item.title: The latest AI-powered martech news and releases
item.description: [MarTech] Zendesk is betting AI agents will handle most customer service interactions, and its $200M-plus Forethought acquisition shows how serious it is. The post The latest AI-powered martech news and releases appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: AI is repricing the marketing stack, not collapsing it
item.description: [MarTech] AI is making coordination tools easier to replicate, putting pricing pressure on parts of the marketing stack that deliver convenience rather than operational risk. The post AI is repricing the marketing stack, not collapsing it appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: China AI startup Moonshot snags funds at US$18 billion valuation
item.description: [Business Times] [BEIJING] Moonshot AI is seeking to raise as much as US$1 billion in an expanded funding round that would value the startup...

item.title: US Commerce Department withdraws planned rule on AI chip exports
item.description: [Business Times] [NEW YORK] The US Commerce Department withdrew a planned rule on artificial intelligence (AI) chip exports on Friday (Mar 13), the latest...

item.title: The price isn’t right: Oil spike fuels risks in South-east Asia’s tech scene
item.description: [Business Times] RISING oil prices triggered by the ongoing war in the Middle East could quickly drive up operating costs within South-east Asia’s tech...

item.title: She spent 16 hours on Instagram in a day. It's up to a jury to decide if Meta is to blame
item.description: [BBC Technology] A landmark lawsuit will set the stage for thousands of people who say social media platforms are intentionally addictive.

item.title: Nvidia’s version of OpenClaw could solve its biggest problem: security
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Nvidia announced an open enterprise AI agent platform, called NemoClaw, that is built off of viral OpenClaw.

item.title: Samsung Elec showcases Nvidia's new inference chip made using 4 nanometer process
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Commentary: Tech companies are blaming massive layoffs on AI. What’s really going on?
item.description: [CNA Tech] Genuine disruption from artificial intelligence is visible in specific corners of the labour market, but the scale of that disruption is commonly overstated, says this professor.

item.title: DLSS 5 looks like a real-time generative AI filter for video games
item.description: [The Verge AI] Nvidia announced DLSS 5 on Monday during its GTC conference, and based on early reactions, it's going to be a divisive update, with some reactions calling it "slop" that unacceptably alters artistic intent. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is calling this the "GPT moment for graphics - blending hand-crafted

item.title: Teens sue Elon Musk’s xAI over Grok’s AI-generated CSAM
item.description: [The Verge AI] Three Tennessee teens are suing Elon Musk's xAI over claims that the company's Grok AI chatbot generated sexualized images and videos of themselves as minors, as reported earlier by The Washington Post. The proposed class action lawsuit, filed on Monday, accuses Musk and other xAI leaders of knowing

item.title: Jensen Huang just put Nvidia’s Blackwell and Vera Rubin sales projections into the $1 trillion stratosphere
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said he expects $1 trillion worth of orders for the chips.

item.title: Nvidia bets on AI inference as chip revenue opportunity hits $1 trillion
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Teens sue Musk's xAI over Grok's pornographic images of them
item.description: [BBC Technology] Musk's AI chatbot has created millions of fake sexualised images, experts say.

item.title: Warren presses Pentagon over decision to grant xAI access to classified networks
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Sen. Elizabeth Warren noted that Grok, xAI's controversial chatbot, has created harmful outputs for users and poses a potential national security risk.

item.title: US stocks close higher as traders return to AI stocks
item.description: [Straits Times] The S&P 500 climbed 1.01 per cent, its strongest one-day gain in over a month, while Nasdaq gained 1.22 per cent.

item.title: Nvidia bets on AI inference as chip revenue opportunity hits $1 trillion
item.description: [Straits Times] It marks a big step up from the around US$500 billion revenue opportunity for 2026 Nvidia reiterated at its last earnings call.

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu is struggling to prove he’s not an AI clone
item.description: [The Verge AI] Social media platforms are currently awash with conspiracy theories claiming that Benjamin Netanyahu has been killed or injured and replaced by AI-generated deepfakes. Between clips that supposedly show the Israeli prime minister sporting extra fingers and drinking from a bottomless, gravity-defying

item.title: Roche ramps up AI computing capacity with Nvidia chip expansion
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Uber, Nvidia plan robotaxi rollout in 28 cities starting next year
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Skild AI, Nvidia deploy robot brain on Blackwell assembly lines
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Memories AI is building the visual memory layer for wearables and robotics
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Memories.ai is building a large visual memory model that can index and retrieve video-recorded memories for physical AI.

item.title: Elon Musk’s xAI faces child porn lawsuit from minors Grok allegedly undressed
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] The three plaintiffs are seeking to represent anyone who had real images of them as a minor altered into sexual content by Grok.

item.title: Nvidia’s DLSS 5 uses generative AI to boost photorealism in video games, with ambitions beyond gaming
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Nvidia’s new DLSS 5 uses generative AI and structured graphics data to make video games more realistic. CEO Jensen Huang says the approach could eventually spread to other industries.

item.title: Encyclopedia Britannica is suing OpenAI for allegedly ‘memorizing’ its content with ChatGPT
item.description: [The Verge AI] On Friday, Encyclopedia Britannica and dictionary publisher Merriam-Webster filed a lawsuit against OpenAI alleging that it used their copyrighted content to train its AI, then generated responses that were "substantially similar" to their content, as previously reported by Reuters. According to Bri

item.title: Yahoo CEO Jim Lanzone on reviving the web’s homepage
item.description: [The Verge AI] Today, I’m talking with Jim Lanzone, who is the CEO of Yahoo. It’s basically impossible to sum up the Yahoo story, but the short version of it is that a long time ago Yahoo paid Google to run the search box on its website, and basically everything has gone sideways since. You’ll hear Jim refer [&#82

item.title: Salesforce Agentforce: What you need to know
item.description: [MarTech] We've updated our page on Agentforce with details of the Agentforce Contact Center release. The post Salesforce Agentforce: What you need to know appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: The new must-haves in B2B ecommerce tech stacks go beyond CRM and CMS
item.description: [MarTech] As B2B buyers expect more B2C-like experiences, ecommerce marketers need tools that go far beyond the basics. The post The new must-haves in B2B ecommerce tech stacks go beyond CRM and CMS appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: 当AI进入真实世界：Hitch Open在F1赛道开启物理智能时代
item.description: [钛媒体] 当人类速度的图腾——F1 方格旗在上海国际赛车场猎猎作响时，一种关于“速度”的定义正在被重新书写。

item.title: This is not a fly uploaded to a computer
item.description: [The Verge AI] Last week, a few posts about a so-called virtual "embodied fly" tore through X, boosted by AI hype accounts and excited commenters who didn't seem to understand what it was they were excited about. The videos came from San Francisco-based Eon Systems, which says it's working toward "digital human in

item.title: 霸王茶姬拟进入韩国市场；爱诗科技完成3亿美元C轮融资，多家海外机构参与投资；完美日记母公司获战略投资，计划扩张海外 ｜一周大公司出海
item.description: [钛媒体] 一文阅读本周大公司出海热点。

item.title: Firms urged to check if other users edited their data on Companies House
item.description: [BBC Technology] The glitch meant logged-in users could view and edit other companies' details without their consent.

item.title: Singapore stocks end higher as Middle East conflict enters third week; STI up 0.6%
item.description: [Straits Times] Bourse operator Singapore Exchange led the STI gainers, rising 2.9 per cent to close at $18.93.

item.title: Australian tea brand T2 Tea shuts all outlets in Singapore
item.description: [Straits Times] The retailer expanded into Asia in 2017 with the opening of its 313@Somerset outlet

item.title: 韶音如何在技术无人区，走出耳机第三条路？
item.description: [钛媒体] OpenFit Pro通过“滤噪算法+超级单元+人体工学”的软硬件结合方案，打破了“开放式耳机无法降噪”的刻板印象。

item.title: 地平线芯片负责人将离职，公司走向软硬一体架构｜36氪独家
item.description: [36氪] 图为 地平线 文｜肖漫编辑｜李勤、杨轩 36氪独家获悉，地平线芯片研发负责人陈鹏即将离职，内部呼声比较高的接替人选是地平线副总裁兼首席架构师苏箐。据悉，地平线内部一直在推动软硬一体，苏箐也参与了征程7系列芯片的架构设计和产品定义。 陈鹏在加入地平线之前已在ICT芯片行业工作了19年，曾管理千人级的芯片团队，是芯片行业的老兵。 加入地平线后，陈鹏主要负责芯片SoC系统架构设计和芯片生产厂商对接等事务，加入地平线时正值征程6系列芯片开发阶段，接手了征程6P从设计到量产到出货的全过程。 征程6P是地平线当前主力产品之一，单颗算力560TOPS，被应用在多家车企的中高阶智能驾驶方案

item.title: 第一批移植AI“器官”的人，正在产生排异反应
item.description: [钛媒体] 身体无所谓、精神受不了。

item.title: 扫地机器人6.0时代，跨界的萤石给出了AI清洁新方案｜AWE 2026
item.description: [钛媒体] 扫地机内卷时代，务实比“虚假繁荣”更能走得长远。

item.title: 零一汽车再获12亿元融资，想成为重卡领域的「特斯拉」｜36氪首发
item.description: [36氪] 零一汽车 文｜肖漫 编辑｜李勤 36氪获悉，新能源智能重卡公司零一汽车已完成新一轮12亿元人民币融资。本轮融资由溥泉资本（宁德时代旗下唯一产业投资平台）、Momenta、蔚来资本联合领投，安徽灵通集团、方广投资、淡马锡旗下毅峰资本、愉悦资本、申能诚毅、蓝湖资本跟投，老股东活水资本持续跟投。 这是零一汽车近半年内完成的第二笔融资。2025年年中，公司刚刚完成 5亿元A轮融资，连续两轮融资金额累计超过17亿元。 成立于2022年的零一汽车，是一家同时布局新能源重卡整车与自动驾驶技术的创业公司。公司由前图森未来联合创始人黄泽铧、前三一重卡总经理、福田戴姆勒副总裁张红松，以及前中国

item.title: 36氪晚报｜韩元对美元汇率收盘价跌至近18年来最低；WTI原油上破100美元/桶；抖音旗下科技公司在深圳成立新信息技术公司
item.description: [36氪] 大公司： 分析人士：虽然涉及华虹的利好传闻不断，英伟达GTC 2026大会可能也是引爆半导体板块的一个主因 3月16日午后，创业板指涨超1%。半导体、存储芯片板块持续走高。其中，总市值超2000亿的华虹公司A股一度大涨超16%，其H股一度大涨超11%。整个半导体板块被带飞，芯片ETF午后直线飙升，一度涨超2%。分析人士认为，虽然涉及华虹的利好传闻不断，英伟达即将召开的GTC2026大会可能也是引爆半导体板块的一个主因。另外，有消息称，联电、世界先进、力积电等晶圆代工厂涨价，涨价也成为另一个驱动因素。（券商中国） 抖音旗下科技公司在深圳成立新信息技术公司 36氪获悉，爱企查App

item.title: Waiters in S’pore among food services workers to get annual pay rise of up to 6.7% for next 3 years
item.description: [Straits Times] About 15,000 full-time food services workers will have their monthly salaries raised to at least $2,220.

item.title: Models Are Applying to Be the Face of AI Scams
item.description: [WIRED AI] Dozens of Telegram channels reviewed by WIRED include job listings for “AI face models.” The (mostly) women who land these gigs are likely being used to dupe victims out of their money.

item.title: Western economies have become dangerously reliant on global supply chains: Donald Trump Jr
item.description: [Straits Times] He called for the rebuilding of US industrial muscle in sectors from energy to semiconductors to strategic minerals.

item.title: 补齐AI板块核心拼图，网络基建「卖铲人」飞速创新登陆港交所
item.description: [36氪] 继2025年港交所重现“IPO大年”（全年117家公司上市，募资总额达2869亿港元，同比大增200%）之后，2026年的港股上市潮仍在强势延续年内已有28家企业成功IPO，募资总额逼近千亿大关。 值得注意的是，今年港股IPO的含“AI”量极高。不仅有MiniMax、智谱等大模型独角兽接连登陆，壁仞科技、天数智芯等算力巨头也相继入局。而在3月13日，港股AI资产池再添核心资产——“面向全球市场的企业网络解决方案第一股”飞速创新顺利通过港交所聆讯，为火热的AI板块补齐了至关重要的“网络基建”拼图。 AI时代的底层基建由算力、存储与网络“三足鼎立”。摩根大通近日研报指出，伴随GPU集群规模

item.title: 清越科技发布双品牌战略，将拓展服务机器人核心部件领域丨最前线
item.description:
[36氪] 作者丨邱晓芬 3月13日，PeroPure清越科技正式宣布旗下两大核心品牌的战略升级——专注家电领域的“配盾”品牌升级为清洁电器全生态供应商、健康家电解决方案定制商；新品牌“Show Robotics“未来将聚焦服务机器人核心功能组件。 PeroPure清越科技起源于2019年的斯坦福大学电化学实验室，在水基电化学领域积累了深厚的技术沉淀与产业经验。
清越科技 在发布会现场，PeroPure清越科技CEO徐蜂凯表示，配盾将彻底打破“单一元器件供应商”的定位，成为清洁电器全生态供应商与健康家电解决方案定制商。 据介绍，依托近百项电化学技术体系，配盾模块以自来水为原料，在

item.title: 车规级碳化硅模块厂商再融资亿元，加速入局AI算力中心基建 | 硬氪首发
item.description: [36氪] 作者 | 乔钰杰 编辑 | 袁斯来 硬氪获悉，高性能SiC（碳化硅）模块厂家无锡利普思半导体有限公司（下称“利普思”）近日完成亿元PreB+轮融资，新投资方为扬州国金、扬州龙投资本。资金将主要用于公司在扬州布局专业化车规级SiC（碳化硅）模块封装测试基地和市场推广。 利普思成立于2019年，是一家专注于第三代功率半导体SiC模块设计、生产与销售的硬科技企业。 功率半导体是控制电能转换的核心器件。与传统IGBT模块相比，SiC模块具有更高耐压、更低损耗和更高开关频率等优势，在新能源汽车主驱、超级快充、储能、电网设备以及AI算力电源等高压高功

item.title: Is this product 'human-made'? The race to establish an AI-free logo
item.description: [BBC Technology] The backlash to the growing use of the tech has led to an explosion in attempts to come up with 'AI-Free' logo that could be used globally.

item.title: Most consumers use AI, but few fully trust it
item.description: [MarTech] New research shows AI is already shaping product discovery and purchases, but a large gap between usage and trust is creating new challenges for marketers. The post Most consumers use AI, but few fully trust it appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Google's AI Search Results Love to Refer You Back to Google
item.description: [WIRED AI] The company's generative AI search tools increasingly cite its own services, like Google Search and YouTube, over third-party publishers.

item.title: China’s OpenClaw Boom Is a Gold Rush for AI Companies
item.description: [WIRED AI] Hype around the open source agent is driving people to rent cloud servers and buy AI subscriptions just to try it, creating a windfall for tech companies.

item.title: Gamers’ Worst Nightmares About AI Are Coming True
item.description: [WIRED AI] From the global RAM shortage driving up console prices to job loss in the industry, gaming is shaping up to be one of the AI boom's biggest casualties.

item.title: Palantir Demos Show How the Military Could Use AI Chatbots to Generate War Plans
item.description: [WIRED AI] Software demos and Pentagon records detail how chatbots like Anthropic’s Claude could help the Pentagon analyze intelligence and suggest next steps.

item.title: AI toys for children misread emotions and respond inappropriately, researchers warn
item.description: [BBC Technology] In first study of its kind, Cambridge researchers found AI toys could misread some children's emotions.
